title: Tax Incentive to Drive Investment in Renewable Hydrogen
content: The Australian Government's Future Made in Australia (Production Tax Credits and Other Measures) Bill 2024 introduces the Hydrogen Production Tax Incentive (HPTI), aimed at accelerating the development of renewable hydrogen. The HPTI will offer a refundable tax offset of $2 per kilogram to eligible producers from 1 July 2027 to 30 June 2040, supporting project development, cost reduction, and scale-building. The initiative is part of the $22.7 billion Future Made in Australia plan and aligns with the 2024 National Hydrogen Strategy, positioning Australia to benefit economically from renewable hydrogen production, including exports and domestic applications like green ammonia and fertilisers.
author: Analysts Account
source: Department of Climate Change, Energy, the Environment and Water, Australia
